skill.name: task-reminder
skill.description: Organize scattered tasks into actionable lists and generate daily/weekly/deadline reminder plans when you need a structured schedule and exportable outputs (MD/CSV), with optional system notifications.
skill.summary: Converts scattered tasks into structured daily, weekly, and deadline-based reminder plans with exportable Markdown and CSV output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 67 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 49 external_commands findings are Markdown code block delimiters (triple backticks) in SKILL.md, not shell execution operators. The 13 weak cryptographic algorithm findings are misidentified ArgumentParser calls in Python and text content like filename extensions in Markdown/JSON files. The filesystem finding is legitimate CSV output for a task management tool. No network code exists in the Python script. No prompt injection was detected. The skill is safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: 49 instances of triple-backtick Markdown code block delimiters in SKILL.md were flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is an AI instruction document that is never executed as shell code. The backtick characters are Markdown syntax, not shell command substitution operators.

low_findings.title: ArgumentParser misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Two ArgumentParser constructor calls in scripts/task_reminder.py:209 and scripts/validate_skill.py:12 were flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms. ArgumentParser is a standard library argument parser, not a cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: Text patterns misidentified as cryptographic operations in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: 8 text segments in SKILL.md were flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are plain documentation text and filename references (e.g., reminders.md), not cryptographic operations. The scanner likely matched on '.md' substring or other text patterns.

low_findings.title: Text patterns misidentified in audit result JSON
low_findings.description: 5 flags in task-reminder_audit_result_v2.json (3 weak crypto algorithm, 2 system reconnaissance). This is a JSON data file containing an archived audit report. It is not executable code and cannot contain cryptographic algorithms or perform reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Source URL citation misidentified as network access
low_findings.description: A GitHub source repository citation link in SKILL.md:7 was flagged as a hardcoded network URL. This is a documentation attribution, not an executable network call. The Python script has no network requests of any kind.

low_findings.title: Legitimate CSV file output for task management
low_findings.description: File write operation at scripts/task_reminder.py:201 was flagged as a filesystem risk. This is the core CSV export feature documented in the skill. The output path is controlled via argparse with a safe default. No path traversal or arbitrary write vulnerability exists.

content.user_title: Organize tasks and generate daily reminders
content.value_statement: Scattered tasks across projects make it hard to know what to work on each day. This skill consolidates your tasks into structured daily, weekly, and deadline-based reminder plans with exportable Markdown and CSV output.
actual_capabilities: Converts raw task lists into structured daily plans for any date range
Generates weekly reminder plans on a configurable day of the week
Highlights tasks approaching deadlines and prioritizes them by urgency
Exports plans to Markdown and CSV files for sharing and collaboration
Accepts tasks through interactive prompt or structured JSON input file
Supports combined output showing daily, weekly, and deadline views
limitations: Cannot send push notifications, email reminders, or system alerts without manual activation
Does not integrate with external calendar services or project management platforms
Relies on local Python execution with no cloud sync or multi-device support
Task priority interpretation depends on user-defined numeric values with no standardized scale

use_cases.title: Student managing assignment deadlines
use_cases.description: A student with multiple lab reports, presentations, and exams due in a month can input all deadlines and receive a day-by-day study plan with priority highlights.

use_cases.title: Freelancer organizing weekly deliverables
use_cases.description: A freelancer managing multiple client projects can generate weekly reminder plans on Monday mornings to review and prioritize upcoming deliverables for the week.

use_cases.title: Team lead creating sprint schedules
use_cases.description: A team lead can input all sprint tasks with deadlines and generate combined daily and weekly plans to share with the team via Markdown or CSV exports.

prompt_templates.title: Quick daily task plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a daily task plan for the next 7 days starting today. I have these tasks: finish quarterly report (deadline in 5 days, priority 4), prepare client presentation (deadline in 3 days, priority 5), review team documents (no deadline, priority 2).
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting a quick day-by-day plan for the upcoming week

prompt_templates.title: Weekly recurring reminders
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up weekly reminders for recurring team tasks. Generate a plan for March 2026 showing every Monday. Tasks: update project status (priority 3, estimate 1h), review pull requests (priority 4, estimate 2h), prepare sprint notes (priority 2, estimate 0.5h).
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up regular weekly check-in reminders

prompt_templates.title: Deadline-based priority plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate deadline-based priority reminders for the next two weeks starting 2026-03-10. Tasks: submit grant proposal due 2026-03-12 (priority 5), complete ethics review due 2026-03-15 (priority 4), draft methodology section due 2026-03-20 (priority 3).
prompt_templates.scenario: Focusing on approaching deadlines to avoid missing due dates

prompt_templates.title: Combined plan with CSV export
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a combined daily and weekly plan for March 15 to March 28, 2026. Use reminder mode all with Tuesday as the weekly review day. Export the schedule to both Markdown and CSV formats. Tasks: literature review (deadline 2026-03-20, priority 4, estimate 8h, tags: research), data analysis (deadline 2026-03-25, priority 5, estimate 12h, tags: analysis), team meeting prep (no deadline, priority 2, estimate 1h, tags: meetings).
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting a comprehensive plan with all reminder types and portable file exports

output_examples.input: I have 5 lab reports due this month and 2 presentations to prepare. Please create a deadline reminder plan.
output_examples.output: A Markdown report listing all 7 tasks sorted by deadline, with each task showing its due date, priority level, estimated time, and tags. Tasks due within 3 days are highlighted. A CSV file with the same schedule is also generated for spreadsheet import.

output_examples.input: Plan my work week of March 1 to March 7 with daily focus items.
output_examples.output: A day-by-day Markdown schedule showing which tasks to focus on each day. Monday through Friday each have 2-3 assigned tasks based on priority, with the highest priority tasks scheduled earliest in the week. Saturdays and Sundays show remaining low-priority items.

output_examples.input: Remind me of approaching deadlines for the next 2 weeks starting March 10.
output_examples.output: A deadline-highlighted report organized by urgency. Tasks are grouped into three sections: due this week, due next week, and further out. Each section lists tasks with their deadline date, days remaining, and priority level. A CSV version is also generated.

best_practices: Use structured JSON input instead of interactive mode for consistent, repeatable results across multiple planning sessions.
Assign clear priority values and deadlines to every task to get the most useful sorting and highlighting in generated plans.
Review the generated reminder files before using them for decision making to verify all tasks and dates are correct.
anti_patterns: Adding vague tasks without deadlines or priority values, which produces flat, unsorted output with no urgency indicators.
Expecting the skill to push notifications or send automated reminders without explicitly enabling notification features.
Using overlapping date ranges across multiple runs, which can produce duplicate reminders for the same tasks.

faq.question: Can this skill send me email or push reminders?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates local files (Markdown and CSV). It does not send email, push notifications, or integrate with messaging platforms.

faq.question: Does the skill support recurring tasks like weekly meetings?
faq.answer: Yes. The weekly reminder mode generates reminders on a specified day each week within the date range. You define which day of the week to use.

faq.question: What task information should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide at minimum a title and deadline for each task. Priority level, estimated hours, and tags help produce more useful sorted and filtered plans.

faq.question: Can I import tasks from a spreadsheet or CSV file?
faq.answer: Not directly. You can use the JSON input format for structured task data. Convert your spreadsheet to JSON first, then provide the file to the script.

faq.question: Does this skill work with calendar applications?
faq.answer: No. The skill outputs standalone Markdown and CSV files. It does not sync with Google Calendar, Outlook, or any calendar application.

faq.question: How do I change the output file names or location?
faq.answer: Use the --out-md and --out-csv command line arguments to specify custom output paths. The defaults are reminders.md and reminders.csv in the current directory.
